title: hedysarum fruticosum subsp. mongolicum
intro: Hedysarum fruticosum subsp. mongolicum, also known as Mongolian sweet pea or Mongolian hedysarum, is a plant species in the legume family. It is a perennial legume native to Mongolia and parts of China, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, and has been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Hedysarum fruticosum subsp. mongolicum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
